title: ZERO.4 Q2.4 seed-2 cumulative replay guard
hypothesis: An immutable six-slice cumulative replay guard can produce a jointly feasible public and promotion result while keeping public replay regression at or below 2%.
rationale: EXP-001 proved transactional mechanics but falsified local one-step replay deltas as a cumulative authority. Q2.4 directly measures baseline-relative functional drift before every commit.

metrics.name: seed_passed
metrics.description: Conjunctive upstream public and promotion decision, encoded as 0 or 1.

metrics.name: committed_updates
metrics.description: Candidates committed after cumulative replay authority.

metrics.name: rejected_candidates
metrics.description: Candidates atomically rolled back by the cumulative guard.

metrics.name: warning_exceedances
metrics.description: Candidates whose cumulative composite replay increase exceeded 1% or was non-finite.

metrics.name: hard_exceedances
metrics.description: Candidates whose cumulative composite replay increase exceeded 1.5% or was non-finite.

metrics.name: max_cumulative_replay_increase
metrics.description: Largest candidate composite replay increase relative to immutable ZERO.3.

metrics.name: jointly_feasible_checkpoint_count
metrics.description: Public checkpoints satisfying quantity and replay gates together.

metrics.name: replication_seeds_opened
metrics.description: Sealed replication seeds opened after the diagnostic.

outcomes.description: The upstream frozen conjunctive public and promotion gates all pass.
Execution completes validly without a seed-level pass.
The process exits unsuccessfully, times out, or violates the metric envelope.
